title: Life and Art by Emma Lazarus — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Not while the fever of the blood is strong,
The heart throbs loud, the eyes are veiled, no less
With passion than with tears, the Muse shall bless
The poet-sould to help and soothe with song.
Not then she bids his trembling lips express
The aching gladness, the voluptuous pain.
Life is his poem then; flesh, sense, and brain
One full-stringed lyre attuned to happiness.
meaning: "Life and Art" by Emma Lazarus is a sad and love and deep and nature poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Not while the fever of the blood is strong, The heart throbs loud, the eyes are veiled, no less...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Emma Lazarus's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
